danger_signals.visual_form: Nazi guards and soldiers rendered as 2D sprites from multiple angles; distinct uniform appearance with recognizable silhouettes
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player; combat required

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated dog sprites with aggressive behavior patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Fast-moving melee threat; environmental danger indicator

danger_signals.visual_form: Final level contains particularly large or visually distinct enemy sprite
danger_signals.meaning: Climactic danger; significant threat requiring all resources

danger_signals.visual_form: Closed door sprites requiring key items to pass
danger_signals.meaning: Progression blocked; must find key or alternate path

danger_signals.visual_form: Health percentage displayed in UI; visual feedback when damaged
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability increasing; risk of death imminent

safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; player can restore safety margin

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapon and ammo sprites placed in accessible locations
safety_signals.meaning: Combat preparedness; reduced vulnerability to threats

safety_signals.visual_form: End-of-level elevator sprite; transition point visually distinct
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression; level completion; respite point

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden rooms revealed by activating wall tiles that slide back
safety_signals.meaning: Bonus resources; shelter from enemy sight lines; optional safety advantage
Death penalty mitigation; safety buffer against failure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nazi military insignia on guard uniforms; swastika potentially visible on environmental props
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Guards and soldiers rendered with distinct 2D sprite poses; dogs as allied military units; boss characters with enhanced visual distinctiveness
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level theming around Nazi bunkers and buildings; architecture suggests German military installations; interior prison/fortress aesthetic

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Player character William 'B.J.' Blazkowicz as Allied spy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Escape-from-captivity premise; Castle Wolfenstein as Nazi stronghold; missions against Nazi positions indicate liberation/sabotage objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level design centered on Nazi military architecture; guard presence throughout; fortress/bunker aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guards patrol and engage player; dogs attack on sight; enemies actively defend territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Elevator at level end serves as escape vehicle; goal-oriented level structure moving toward exit
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player traversal toward elevator; enemy opposition intensifies as player progresses deeper into castle

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final level features visually distinct boss enemy; potentially enhanced size or detail compared to standard sprites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss exhibits more aggressive patterns; requires sustained combat; defeating boss triggers progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon and health pickup density varies by level; scattered throughout environment at deliberate intervals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must manage ammunition and health carefully in early levels; later levels may provide greater abundance

environmental_storytelling.example: Each level designed as Nazi bunker or military installation; environment itself communicates enemy faction control and player objective (escape from fortified position)
Guards positioned at choke points, corridors, and strategic locations; dog placements in tight spaces; suggests military defensive strategy
Elevator sprite as end-of-level goal provides clear visual target; player always knows where to progress toward
Ammo and health placed along natural navigation paths; encourages exploration; scattered treasures reward thorough level search

Episode titles ('Escape from Castle Wolfenstein,' 'Operation: Eisenfaust,' 'Die, Führer, Die!') communicate narrative progression from escape to offensive operations; visual level sequencing reinforces story arc

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves in predictable patrol patterns; sprite shows forward-facing stance
Faces player; fires weapon; sprite animates shooting pose; moves toward player position
Fast sprite animation; aggressive charging behavior; leaps at player; high damage output despite smaller visual size
Enhanced sprite size/detail; sustained attack pattern; requires multiple hits to defeat; triggers level completion upon death
Full movement capability; aggressive engagement possible; resource expenditure acceptable
Vulnerability indicated by UI; evasion behavior encouraged; resource preservation prioritized

notes: Wolfenstein 3D uses minimalist but effective visual communication for a 1992 FPS. The 2.5D sprite system (3D environment with 2D entities) constrains manifestation options, but the game compensates through consistent enemy sprite design, architectural theming, and clear visual hierarchy. Nazi faction identity is established primarily through uniform design, environmental architecture (bunkers/fortresses), and narrative framing rather than color coding or complex sigils. World state changes are communicated through enemy density, boss encounters, and environmental progression rather than dynamic visual shifts. The game's manifestation language is direct: enemies are obvious threats; pickups are obvious resources; the elevator is the obvious goal.
